display_name: Ceph RADOS Gateway (RGW) / Rook-Ceph Object Store
ai_scope_discriminator: Confirm AI/ML artifact storage from the bucket/container NAMES only, never object bodies. Anonymous GET / on a public-policy gateway returns <ListAllMyBucketsResult> whose <Bucket><Name> entries reveal intent: mlflow, mlflow-artifacts, models, model-registry, checkpoints, datasets, training-data, artifacts, dvc, lakefs, weights, embeddings, feast, kubeflow-pipelines, sagemaker, huggingface-cache, tensorboard. On an anon-readable bucket, GET /{bucket}?list-type=2 returns <ListBucketResult> whose <Contents><Key> values are the manifest: keys ending .ckpt .safetensors .pt .pth .bin .gguf .onnx .pb .h5 .npz, MLmodel / conda.yaml / model.pkl (MLflow run layout), .dvc pointers, or lakeFS _lakefs/ refs. Swift path /swift/v1 lists containers with the same naming tells. Reading the bucket list and key list is in-scope name enumeration; fetching any <Key> body is exfil and out of scope.
fingerprint_markers: Server response header: "Ceph Object Gateway (RELEASE_NAME)" (e.g. "Ceph Object Gateway (squid)"/(reef)/(quincy)/(pacific)) set in src/rgw/rgw_rest.cc; overridable via rgw_service_provider_name

x-amz-request-id header value formatted tx<hex>-<hex>-<hex>-<zonegroup> with a trailing zone suffix (commonly "-default"); the tx-prefix + trailing zone segment distinguishes RGW from AWS S3 and MinIO request IDs
Anonymous/authed GET / returns S3 XML root <ListAllMyBucketsResult xmlns="http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/"> with <Owner><DisplayName> and <Buckets>
GET /{bucket} returns <ListBucketResult> with <Contents><Key>/<ETag>/<StorageClass>STANDARD and RGW-specific <Type>Normal|Appendable element absent from AWS S3
Swift API reachable at /swift/v1 (rgw_swift_url_prefix default "swift"); /swift/v1/info exposes capabilities JSON when swift_auth API enabled
Admin Ops API mounted at /admin/* (rgw_enable_apis default includes "admin"); /admin/metadata/bucket and /admin/usage require admin caps but the path returns RGW-specific AccessDenied XML
Beast frontend default bind port 7480 (rgw_frontends "beast port=7480"); Rook-Ceph CephObjectStore gateway runs internally on port 80, exposed via Service/NodePort/Ingress on 80/443
fp_guards: Do NOT match on <ListAllMyBucketsResult>/<ListBucketResult> XML alone: MinIO, SeaweedFS, Garage, OpenStack Swift-S3, AWS S3 and Cloudflare R2 all emit the same S3 schema. Require the Server header to contain "Ceph Object Gateway" OR the x-amz-request-id to carry the tx...-<zone> RGW format.

MinIO sets Server: MinIO and x-amz-request-id as a single uppercase hex token with no tx prefix and no trailing zone segment, and adds x-minio-* / x-ratelimit headers; exclude any host whose headers contain "MinIO" or x-minio-.

Generic AWS S3 / R2 lack a self-identifying Server header (AmazonS3 / cloudflare) and have no Swift /swift/v1 or /admin/* surface; presence of /swift/v1/info JSON or RGW <Type> element in object listings disambiguates to Ceph.

A 403 AccessDenied to anonymous GET / is the DEFAULT secure posture, not an exposure; only count <ListAllMyBucketsResult> or a 200 <ListBucketResult> with <Contents> as anon-listable. SignatureDoesNotMatch/AccessDenied = surface open, access not exercised.
Behind a CDN/LB the Server header is frequently stripped or rewritten; fall back to the x-amz-request-id tx-zone format and the /swift/v1 + /admin path probes before claiming RGW.
Honeypot/deception fleets can spoof the Server string; cross-confirm with a second RGW-unique signal (Swift /swift/v1/info JSON shape or the <Type>Normal element) before labeling CONFIRMED.

cves.desc: RadosGW OIDC/STS JWT authentication bypass: a token with alg:none skips signature verification, letting an attacker forge claims and assume roles. CVSS 8.1. Affects Ceph <= 19.2.3 (Squid). Relevant where rgw STS/OIDC is enabled in front of model/artifact buckets.
RGW crash (DoS) triggered by a misconfigured CORS rule, allowing an attacker to take the gateway offline.
RGW improperly verifies POST policy keys, enabling bypass of intended form-POST upload restrictions on a bucket.
ceph-crash / RGW privilege escalation via insecure handling, local-context flaw in the Ceph stack.
CRLF / HTTP header injection in radosgw via the CORS ExposeHeader handling, enabling response splitting against RGW clients.
Unauthenticated DoS: a Swift GET to a URL ending in two slashes crashes radosgw (fixed in 14.2.21). Directly reachable on the anonymous /swift/v1 surface.
HTTP response header injection via the CORS ExposeHeader tag in radosgw, allowing header smuggling into S3 responses.

pivot_paths: GET / -> read <ListAllMyBucketsResult> bucket names only (intent enumeration; no object fetch)
GET /{bucket}?list-type=2&max-keys=1000 -> read <Contents><Key> manifest of an anon-readable bucket (key names only)
GET /{bucket}?versions / ?uploads -> object-version and multipart manifest names (no body download)
GET /swift/v1 -> Swift container list when swift API enabled; GET /swift/v1/info -> capability JSON
GET /swift/v1/{container} -> container object listing where X-Container-Read .r:* is set (names only)
HEAD / and HEAD /{bucket} -> Server + x-amz-request-id confirmation without transferring a listing body
GET /admin/usage and /admin/metadata/bucket -> probe whether Admin Ops API is anon-reachable (read-only; expect AccessDenied on a hardened host)
GET /{bucket}/?policy and /?acl -> read the effective bucket policy / ACL document to confirm the AllUsers / Principal:* misconfiguration that produced the exposure
restraint_note: Names ARE the finding. Enumerate bucket/container names and object KEYS only; never download an object body, an MLflow MLmodel/conda.yaml file, a .ckpt/.safetensors weight, or any dataset blob. Do not write, delete, abort multipart uploads, or alter any bucket policy/ACL. Do not exercise CVE-2024-48916 JWT forgery or any DoS CVE against a live target. A 403/AccessDenied is logged as \"surface open, access not exercised,\" not as access. Sample at most one tiny key listing to confirm AI-artifact intent, then stop.
